entries.title: OpenClaw's ClawHub skill marketplace: an agentic supply-chain attack surface
entries.summary: Unit 42 found five evasive malicious 'skills' persisting on ClawHub — the marketplace OpenClaw's agent pulls third-party, markdown-driven packages from with broad local system access — despite VirusTotal + ClawScan screening added after February's incidents. The five span infostealers (macOS, C2), scanner evasion (inflated file size to exceed thresholds), and two NOVEL agentic threats: runtime agentic affiliate injection and agentic front-running.
entries.takeaway: Agent skill marketplaces are a critical, largely-untrusted link in the software supply chain — marketplace scanning alone does not make them safe.

lessons.point: Agent skills are broad-access packages; the marketplace is a supply-chain link to treat as untrusted
lessons.excerpt: markdown-driven packages with broad local system access, making ClawHub a critical link in the agentic software supply chain

lessons.point: Malware evades marketplace scanners with simple tricks (oversized files exceed scan thresholds)
lessons.excerpt: inflated file size to exceed scanner thresholds, bypassing both ClawScan and VirusTotal detection

lessons.point: New 'agentic' threat classes are emerging — affiliate injection and front-running done at agent runtime
lessons.excerpt: runtime agentic affiliate injection and agentic front-running. Both are novel techniques

actionable.title: Vet and sandbox agent skills
actionable.detail: Pin and review third-party agent skills from trusted publishers, sandbox their execution with least-privilege, and don't rely on marketplace scanning — add your own allow-list + runtime policy.
